results.norm: Prefer norms robust to strategic compliance. Norms must be justifiable to all parties under uncertainty about their relative strengths, and are retroactively auditable against this standard including the norm-development deliberation itself. The competitive structure is legitimate only if it incentivizes genuine contribution, not performance of contribution.
atomic_norms: Prefer norms robust to strategic compliance.
Norms must be justifiable to all parties under uncertainty about their relative strengths, and are retroactively auditable against this standard including the norm-development deliberation itself.
The competitive structure is legitimate only if it incentivizes genuine contribution, not performance of contribution.

results.norm: Violation-naming is protected when genuine and itself a violation when strategic. Norms may be revised only on structural evidence demonstrable, repeatable misalignment between a norm's operation and its stated purpose, verifiable to a neutral observer and indexed to the norm's own goals, not merely outcomes that disadvantage the complaining agent.
atomic_norms: Violation-naming is protected when genuine and itself a violation when strategic.

Norms may be revised only on structural evidence demonstrable, repeatable misalignment between a norm's operation and its stated purpose, verifiable to a neutral observer and indexed to the norm's own goals, not merely outcomes that disadvantage the complaining agent.

results.norm: Before allocation is known, and before seeing others' assessments, each agent makes explicit reasoned assessments of all contributions, including identifying at least one contribution by another agent they consider more valuable than one of their own. These assessments constitute an auditable record for evaluation purposes; they do not determine allocation directly.
atomic_norms: Before allocation is known, and before seeing others' assessments, each agent makes explicit reasoned assessments of all contributions, including identifying at least one contribution by another agent they consider more valuable than one of their own. These assessments constitute an auditable record for evaluation purposes; they do not determine allocation directly.

results.norm: Competitive structure legitimate only if it incentivizes genuine contribution
atomic_norms: Competitive structure legitimate only if it incentivizes genuine contribution

results.norm: Agents must show their reasoning, not just conclusions; make claims falsifiable where possible; and actively surface implicit constraints. Strategic positioning, padding, and withholding of relevant information are prohibited.
atomic_norms: Agents must show their reasoning, not just conclusions; make claims falsifiable where possible; and actively surface implicit constraints. Strategic positioning, padding, and withholding of relevant information are prohibited.

results.norm: Explicit attribution and dependency acknowledgment: When a contribution builds on another agent's work, that dependency must be stated. Foundational contributions that structure subsequent work must be traced at synthesis.
atomic_norms: Explicit attribution and dependency acknowledgment: When a contribution builds on another agent's work, that dependency must be stated. Foundational contributions that structure subsequent work must be traced at synthesis.

results.norm: Agents evaluate contributions they did not make and flag conflicts of interest. Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect.
atomic_norms: Agents evaluate contributions they did not make and flag conflicts of interest. Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect.

results.norm: Negative contributions (ruling out paths), reframing, and question-identification count as substantive. Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; if neither party can produce one, the contribution is provisionally credited.
atomic_norms: Negative contributions (ruling out paths), reframing, and question-identification count as substantive.
Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; if neither party can produce one, the contribution is provisionally credited.

results.norm: Process integrity: Norm revision requires supermajority agreement. Non-contribution is presumptively neutral unless an agent makes a late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier. Collaborative exchanges may be credited jointly, with individual contributions within the exchange remaining visible and each agent retaining a floor of individual credit.
atomic_norms: Non-contribution is presumptively neutral unless an agent makes a late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier.
Collaborative exchanges may be credited jointly, with individual contributions within the exchange remaining visible and each agent retaining a floor of individual credit.

results.norm: Evaluation criteria specified in advance: Meta-criteria and the identity and process of final evaluation must be agreed upon before substantive work begins and cannot be changed after work has started. External evaluators receive the meta-criteria framework before evaluation begins.
atomic_norms: Evaluation criteria specified in advance: Meta-criteria and the identity and process of final evaluation must be agreed upon before substantive work begins and cannot be changed after work has started. External evaluators receive the meta-criteria framework before evaluation begins.
